task: PR C1.9 — Backward-compat PROD-read-only verification (the apply gate)
source: NEW expansion derived from LOCAL real expand_cultural_heritage(_alias_cultural_heritage(...)) (C1.2 applied locally); counts run READ-ONLY against PROD mcp__supabase-remote.
method: For each of the 31 baseline options, the NEW expanded label-set was produced by the REAL deployed-locally function path, exactly as search_lessons feeds it: expand_cultural_heritage(_alias_cultural_heritage(ARRAY[slug])). The tree (71-row cultural_heritage_hierarchy) is data-independent, so the local function yields the exact label-set the rebuilt PROD filter will match on.

Cross-checked 5 representative options (asian, african-american, indigenous-and-diaspora, levantine, north-american) against an independent WITH RECURSIVE CTE over the seed table — all sets_match = true (no local-migration drift).

NEW count per option = SELECT count(*) FROM lessons WHERE cultural_heritage && <expanded labels> AND retired_at IS NULL — the SAME match path the C1.0 baseline used (faithful to search_lessons' WHERE: `l.cultural_heritage && expanded_cultures AND l.retired_at IS NULL`).
OLD count per option = baseline `countToday` from heritage-filter-baseline.json (the live `&&` heritage path on the OLD 6-row flat table + one-tier expand).

sanityAnchors.note: PROD anchors match the C1.0 baseline exactly (767 / 788 / 339) — same corpus, apples-to-apples.

gateVerdict.rule: Every one of the 31 options must return same-or-greater than the C1.0 baseline (never fewer).
gateVerdict.result: PASS — with one documented correction (USER-APPROVED 2026-06-16, Session 16). 21 HEAL, 9 SAME, and 1 numeric -1 (mediterranean 45->44) that is a vocab-aligned correction (Moroccan re-homed to North African, not Mediterranean), NOT lesson loss. Executor correctly stopped on the decrease per the hard boundary; supervisor re-verified root cause against live PROD; user approved recording PASS-with-correction.
gateVerdict.decreaseDetail: mediterranean 45 -> 44 (-1). ROOT CAUSE: the OLD 6-row flat table cross-parented 'Moroccan' under BOTH 'Mediterranean' and 'African' (and carried phantom 'Turkish'). The NEW vocab.json single-source-of-truth tree parents 'Moroccan' correctly under north-african -> african only (NOT Mediterranean) and has zero 'Turkish' nodes. The single affected lesson — 'Moroccan Carrot Salad, Two Ways' (1LyuuuF-GNwUfVIgxrR_3NQLCEbD59GkfFN145WeTk3Q, tagged [Moroccan, North African, African]) — is NOT lost: it still surfaces under `african` (43->43) and `north-african` (0->3, it is one of those 3). This is the correction of a factually-wrong OLD cross-parenting, not lesson loss / filter narrowing. Per the C1.9 hard boundary (any decrease => STOP + report), this is flagged for supervisor adjudication rather than auto-classified PASS.
gateVerdict.noLessonLoss: No lesson is removed from the heritage filter system by the rebuild. The mediterranean -1 reflects ONE lesson re-homed from a wrong parent to its correct parent; it remains reachable via african / north-african.

options.label: Mediterranean
options.decreaseNote: OLD table wrongly cross-parented Moroccan under Mediterranean; new vocab tree homes Moroccan under north-african. Affected lesson 'Moroccan Carrot Salad, Two Ways' still reachable via african / north-african. Correction, not loss.
